title: TH Densitometry
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"bepuv","text":"This protocol is used to quantify the density of dopaminergic projections to the striatum.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Take 4 cryosections to equally sample the striatum and perform diaminobenzidine staining with a tyrosine hydroxylase antibody. Note: I take every 12th section from Bregma 1.18mm to -0.22mm
Capture brightfield images of the dorsolateral striatum and convert to greyscale images.
Take 20 intensity measurements of the striatum and 5 intensity measurements of the corpus callosum (to account for background staining).
Average the corpus callosum intensities then subtract that from the striatal intensities then average the normalized striatal intensitied.
creator: Haley Geertsma
